name: Modal (modal.com) — GPU training backend
role: Runs the multi-agent coordination training (kinematic JAX env): parallel rollout workers + central learner, checkpointing, scale.
decision: USE Modal for the training core. Native fan-out (.spawn_map, up to 1M pending) + modal.Queue/modal.Dict for the rollout-worker/learner fabric + modal.Volume (~2.5GB/s) for checkpoints; 24h/call (resume for longer). Because training is the JAX kinematic env, ANY CUDA GPU works (A100/H100 fine) — no RT Cores needed, so training is cheap and unconstrained.
gpu_note: Training is pure JAX/CUDA on the kinematic env -> any GPU (A100 ~$2.10-2.50/h, H100 ~$3.95, L40S ~$1.95). The RT-Cores constraint only applies to the Isaac DEMO render, which runs on a separate RTX/L40S box (not necessarily Modal).
topology_example: with modal.Queue.ephemeral() as samples, modal.Dict.ephemeral() as weights:
# learner pulls samples from queue, pushes weights to dict/volume
risks: free tier ~$30/mo is thin for heavy RL -> apply to startup credits; the kinematic env trains in minutes-to-hours so cost stays low
24h per-call cap -> checkpoint+resume for longer runs
if you also render the Isaac demo on Modal, test vulkaninfo on an L40S container first
